actual_events.text: Alphonso Davies enters the fray — Canada's captain joins a tight game
Eustáquio controls a cleared cross with his chest, steadies himself, and arrows a precise strike into the bottom corner from the edge of the box. Canada lead 1-0!

actual_stats.result_note: Canada's late sucker-punch ends South Africa's debut R32 run. Historic win for Canada — first-ever WC knockout-stage victory.

actual_events.text: Sano pounces on a sloppy Casemiro back-pass and slots past Alisson. Japan lead 1-0!
Casemiro atones for his first-half error — arriving late at the back post to head in. 1-1.
90 minutes played — level at 1-1. Six minutes of stoppage time added.
Guimarães plays Martinelli through on the left; he curls a composed finish into the far corner. Brazil win 2-1 — no extra time needed!

actual_stats.result_note: Brazil came from behind — a lethargic first half followed by a dominant second. Martinelli's 96th-minute curler sealed it before extra time.

actual_events.text: Enciso meets Galarza's cross with a glancing header — Paraguay shock Germany 1-0 just before half-time!
Havertz heads past keeper Orlando Gill from close range. Germany draw level — 1-1.
Full time 1-1. Onto extra time.
Jonathan Tah's extra-time goal disallowed by VAR — Waldemar Anton penalised for blocking foul on keeper Gill. Germany furious.
Extra time ends 1-1. Penalty shootout.
Penalty shootout — Paraguay 4-3 Germany. GK Orlando Gill saves Havertz's opening kick. Tah sends his pen over the bar. José Canale scores the winner for Paraguay!

actual_stats.result_note: One of the tournament's biggest shocks. Germany dominated but Orlando Gill's shootout heroics and a VAR drama gave Paraguay an historic win over the favourites.

actual_events.text: Gakpo breaks the deadlock — Netherlands lead 1-0 with just over 15 minutes to play.
Diop rises to head home in stoppage time — Morocco equalise 1-1! Estadio BBVA erupts.
Full time 1-1. Extra time.
Extra time ends goalless. Penalty shootout.
Morocco win 3-2 on penalties. Summerville's decisive kick saved by Bounou. Saibari slots the winner — Morocco advance!

actual_stats.result_note: Morocco's last-gasp Diop header forced extra time, then their nerve in the shootout knocked out the Netherlands. Bounou's save from Summerville was the decisive moment.

actual_events.text: Nusa curls a stunning strike into the top corner — Norway lead 0-1!
Diallo skips past two defenders and slots left-footed past Nyland. 1-1!
Berge's cross finds Haaland unmarked at the back post — he taps in Norway's historic winner. 1-2! Norway's first ever World Cup knockout-stage win.

actual_stats.result_note: Haaland's 86th-minute tap-in ends Ivory Coast's run. Norway advance to face Brazil — Haaland now has 5 goals in 3 World Cup games.

actual_events.text: Mbappé curls a clinical finish into the bottom corner just before the whistle — France lead 1-0 at half-time!
Barcola bursts in behind the Swedish defence and slots past Olsen. France 2-0 — the tie is all but over.
Mbappé completes his brace with a composed finish — France cruise into the Round of 16. 3-0!

actual_stats.result_note: France were ruthless and clinical. Mbappé's brace — either side of Barcola's strike — sealed a dominant 3-0 win. Sweden barely threatened throughout. France face Paraguay in the Round of 16.

actual_events.text: Quiñones curls a stunning right-footed finish into the top corner from the left side of the box. Estadio Azteca erupts — Mexico lead 1-0!
Quiñones finds Jiménez in space inside the box — the veteran striker makes no mistake. Mexico 2-0, and the tie is all but over before half-time.

actual_stats.result_note: Mexico were dominant on home soil, scoring twice inside 31 minutes. Quiñones was outstanding — goal and assist — while Jiménez led the line superbly. Ecuador barely threatened throughout. El Tri advance to face England in the Round of 16.

actual_events.text: Cipenga fires low past Pickford from Mbemba's cross — DR Congo stun England 1-0!
Gordon lifts a cross from the left and Kane meets it with a precise header — England level at 1-1!
Kane receives on the edge of the box, creates space and thunders a drive into the top corner — England win 2-1!

actual_stats.result_note: DR Congo led for 68 minutes but Kane's late brace clinched England's Round of 16 berth. Gordon was the creator for both goals.

actual_events.text: Diarra breaks the deadlock — Senegal lead Belgium 1-0 after a lengthy spell of possession.
Sarr doubles Senegal's lead — the Lions of Teranga are cruising at 2-0.
Lukaku pulls one back with four minutes to go — Belgium 1-2!
Tielemans headers home to level at 2-2 in stoppage time — Belgium force extra time!
Full time 2-2. Extra time.
Tielemans converts the controversial VAR-awarded penalty in the dying seconds of extra time — Belgium win 3-2! The latest winning goal in World Cup history at 120+5.

actual_stats.result_note: Belgium pulled off the greatest comeback of the tournament — 2-0 down with four minutes left, Lukaku and Tielemans combined to force extra time before Tielemans' 120+5 penalty sealed a dramatic win.

actual_events.text: Balogun pounces on a loose ball and nutmegs Vasilj just before the break — USMNT lead 1-0!
Balogun is shown a straight red card after VAR review for raking his studs down Muharemović's leg — USA down to 10 men.
Tillman curls a brilliant free kick into the top corner — 10-man USA double their lead! 2-0.

actual_stats.result_note: Balogun scored and was then sent off for a controversial red card, but 10-man USA held firm. Tillman's 82nd-minute free kick sealed a historic win — the USMNT's first World Cup knockout victory since 2002.

actual_events.text: Oyarzabal opens the scoring — Cucurella's driving run creates the chance and Oyarzabal finishes clinically. Spain lead 1-0!
Porro powers a header through traffic from Baena's pinpoint cross — Spain double their lead. 2-0!
Oyarzabal slots into the bottom corner from Cucurella's searching pass — his brace wraps up a dominant Spain victory. 3-0!

actual_stats.result_note: Spain were imperious — Oyarzabal's brace and Porro's header sealed a 3-0 win. An early Cucurella goal was ruled out; Schlager made two fine saves to limit the damage before half-time. Spain's first World Cup knockout win since their 2010 triumph.

actual_events.text: Perišić attacks Stanišić's cross at the back post and volleys past Diogo Costa — Croatia lead 0-1!
VAR awards a penalty after Renato Veiga is fouled in the box. Ronaldo steps up — his first-ever goal in a World Cup knockout match — and converts emphatically. 1-1!
Leão whips in a cross from the left and Ramos outjumps two defenders to head into the bottom corner — Portugal win it in stoppage time! 2-1!

actual_stats.result_note: A VAR-filled classic in Toronto — three goals were ruled out for offside before Ronaldo's historic penalty equalised and Ramos headed home a dramatic 94th-minute winner. Ronaldo finally scores in a World Cup knockout round at his fifth attempt. Portugal advance to face Spain in the Round of 16.

actual_events.text: Manzambi drives forward on a brilliant individual run and squares to Embolo, who finishes calmly into the bottom corner. Switzerland lead!
Within 48 seconds of the restart, Algeria fail to clear their lines and Ndoye pounces to double Switzerland's lead. Game effectively over.

actual_stats.result_note: Switzerland's first World Cup knockout win in 88 years. Dominant from the start, they advance to the R16 for a fourth successive World Cup.

actual_events.text: Hafez whips in a free-kick routine delivery and Ashour rises highest to head Egypt in front. 1-0!

Hany tries to head a high ball clear inside his own box but only steers it into his own net. Australia level it up — 1-1. Hany becomes the first player in World Cup history with two own goals in the same tournament.
Full time 1-1. Onto extra time.
Extra time ends 1-1. Penalty shootout.

Penalty shootout — Egypt win 4-2. Mathew Ryan, on as a shootout substitute for Australia, fails to save a single kick. Mohamed Salah's cheeky Panenka seals it — Egypt's first-ever World Cup knockout win!

actual_stats.result_note: Egypt survive a five-goal shootout after Emam Ashour's header was cancelled out by a Mohamed Hany own goal. First-ever World Cup knockout win for the Pharaohs — they advance to the Round of 16.

actual_events.text: Martínez plays a pass into the box and Messi controls it with the outside of his boot before flicking it past Vozinha. Argentina lead 1-0!
Duarte drills a low shot beyond Emiliano Martínez — Cape Verde shock Argentina, level at 1-1!
Full time 1-1. Onto extra time.
A Messi corner is headed clear to the edge of the box — Martínez takes a touch and blasts a left-footed shot in from a tight angle. Argentina lead 2-1 early in extra time!
An incredible strike — Lopes Cabral curls a 26-yard effort into the top corner. Cape Verde level again at 2-2, refusing to go quietly!
Cristian Romero rises for a Messi set piece and his header deflects in off Diney Borges. Argentina 3-2 — the reigning champions survive a huge scare.

actual_stats.result_note: Messi's 20th World Cup goal put Argentina ahead, but 40-year-old keeper Vozinha and a fearless Cape Verde side pushed the champions to the brink before Romero's deflected header settled it in extra time. Argentina advance to face Egypt in the R16.

actual_events.text: Arias fires Colombia into an early lead — 1-0!

actual_stats.result_note: Jhon Arias's early strike was enough — Colombia see out a tight, low-scoring win. Ghana's World Cup campaign ends here; Colombia advance to face Switzerland in the R16.

actual_events.text: Ismael Saibari — Morocco's in-form talisman, a 9.9/10 form rating coming in — goes down injured and has to be replaced by Soufiane Rahimi.
Ounahi curls a free-kick from outside the box into the bottom right — Morocco lead 1-0!
Díaz splits Canada's stretched defence and picks out Ounahi, who sweeps a first-time finish into the top right — Morocco 2-0, and into the quarter-finals.
Díaz races into space and squares for Rahimi, who finishes low into the bottom right in stoppage time — 3-0. Díaz's second assist of the match, a tournament-record fourth overall.

actual_stats.result_note: Rahimi's injury-time introduction for the stricken Saibari proved decisive — he scored Morocco's third after Ounahi's brace either side of the break. Brahim Díaz's two assists took his tournament tally to four, an African record. Canada, who had chances of their own (10 shots to Morocco's 5), never found a way past Bounou.

actual_events.text: Mbappé converts the penalty won by Désiré Doué and confirmed by VAR — his 70th-minute strike, in stifling Philadelphia heat, is enough to send France through. His 19th World Cup goal draws him level with Messi atop the Golden Boot on 7.

actual_stats.result_note: France dominated the ball (76% possession, 15 shots to 5) but a disciplined, physical Paraguay held out until Mbappé's 70th-minute penalty, won by Doué and confirmed by VAR. Goalkeeper Orlando Gill kept it close with a string of second-half saves, including a stoppage-time double stop from Mbappé. France advance to face Morocco in the quarter-finals.

actual_events.text: Schjelderup, on at the break, whips in a left-wing cross and Haaland gets across his marker to break the deadlock — Norway lead 1-0.

Haaland drives in from the left and rifles a low shot into the corner — his second of the night, again teed up by Schjelderup, puts Norway 2-0 up and level with Messi and Mbappé atop the Golden Boot on 7.
Neymar converts a stoppage-time penalty, won after an elbow on Casemiro — academic by then. It proves to be his final touch for the Seleção.

actual_stats.result_note: Brazil were wasteful — Bruno Guimarães had a first-half penalty saved by Ørjan Nyland after a VAR review — and paid for it as second-half substitute Andreas Schjelderup teed up both Haaland goals either side of the 80th minute. Neymar's stoppage-time penalty was too little, too late; this marks Brazil's earliest World Cup exit since 1990, and Neymar's final match for the Seleção. Norway reach a first-ever quarter-final.

actual_events.text: Bellingham strikes to give England an early lead in a hostile Azteca.
Bellingham scores again just 98 seconds after his first — a stunning quickfire double stuns the 80,000+ crowd. England 2-0 up.
Quiñones pulls one back for Mexico just before the break — 2-1.
Quansah is sent off after a VAR review for a high challenge on Jesús Gallardo — England down to 10 men.
Kane converts from the spot, awarded after a foul on Anthony Gordon, to restore England's two-goal cushion despite the man disadvantage — 3-1.
Jiménez converts a penalty of his own to set up a grandstand finish — 3-2.

actual_stats.result_note: Bellingham's 98-second brace either side of the half-hour set the tone, and Kane's penalty — scored just minutes after Quansah's red card — proved decisive. Mexico, roared on by 80,000+ at the Azteca, threw everything at a 10-man England for the final 21 minutes plus 11 of stoppage time, but Jordan Pickford and the England defence held out. England become the first team to beat Mexico in a World Cup at the Azteca.

actual_events.text: Nuno Mendes hits the crossbar after his shot deflects off Pedro Porro — Portugal's best chance goes begging.

Substitute Merino takes a pass from fellow substitute Torres on the edge of the box and finishes low into the left corner — the only goal of the game, deep into stoppage time. Ronaldo's World Cup career ends here; Portugal boss Roberto Martínez resigns immediately after.

actual_stats.result_note: A tense, cagey Dallas-area classic decided in the 91st minute — Rodri (Man of the Match) marshalled Spain's midfield throughout, and substitutes Torres and Merino combined for the winner right at the death. Portugal's Ronaldo, who had confirmed this would be his last World Cup, departs without a goal in the knockout rounds; manager Roberto Martínez quit on the spot. Spain advance to face Belgium in the quarter-finals.

actual_events.text: De Ketelaere opens the scoring early — Belgium ahead inside 10 minutes.
Tillman pulls one back for the hosts — 1-1.
De Ketelaere's second of the match restores Belgium's lead before the half-hour mark — 2-1.
Vanaken, on for the injured Onana in the 21st minute, extends Belgium's lead — 3-1.
Substitute Lukaku caps a dominant Belgian display in stoppage time — 4-1. The United States become the last co-host eliminated, after Canada and Mexico.

actual_stats.result_note: Man of the Match Charles De Ketelaere's early brace set the tone and Belgium were comfortably the better side throughout, with Vanaken and a late Lukaku strike turning it into a rout. The United States — the final co-host standing after Canada and Mexico's exits — never got a foothold beyond Tillman's leveller. Belgium advance to face Spain in the quarter-finals.
